type_check_defn.solution:
(* Manual grade exercise: complete the type_check function definition.
   type_check Gamma t :=
     match t with
     | tm_var x => Gamma x
     | <{\x:T, t1}> => match type_check (x |-> T ; Gamma) t1 with
                        | Some T1 => Some (T -> T1)
                        | None => None
                        end
     | <{t1 t2}> => match type_check Gamma t1, type_check Gamma t2 with
                    | Some (T1 -> T2), Some T3 => if eqb_ty T1 T3 then Some T2 else None
                    | _, _ => None
                    end
     | <{true}> => Some Bool
     | <{false}> => Some Bool
     | <{if t1 then t2 else t3}> => match type_check Gamma t1, type_check Gamma t2, type_check Gamma t3 with
                                    | Some Bool, Some T2, Some T3 => if eqb_ty T2 T3 then Some T2 else None
                                    | _, _, _ => None
                                    end
     end. *)
type_check_defn.explanation: A decidable type-checker that returns Some T if the term has type T, or None if it's ill-typed.

ext_type_checking_sound.solution:
Theorem type_checking_sound : forall Gamma t T,
  type_check Gamma t = Some T -> Gamma |-- t \in T.
Proof.
  intros Gamma t. generalize dependent Gamma.
  induction t; intros Gamma T Htc; simpl in Htc.
  - (* var *) apply T_Var. assumption.
  - (* app *)
    destruct (type_check Gamma t1) eqn:Ht1; try discriminate.
    destruct t; try discriminate.
    destruct (type_check Gamma t2) eqn:Ht2; try discriminate.
    destruct (eqb_ty t3 t) eqn:Heq; try discriminate.
    injection Htc as Htc. subst.
    apply eqb_ty_true in Heq. subst.
    eapply T_App; [apply IHt1 | apply IHt2]; reflexivity.
  - (* abs *)
    destruct (type_check (s |-> t ; Gamma) t0) eqn:Ht0; try discriminate.
    injection Htc as Htc. subst.
    apply T_Abs. apply IHt0. reflexivity.
  - (* true *) injection Htc as Htc. subst. apply T_True.
  - (* false *) injection Htc as Htc. subst. apply T_False.
  - (* if *)
    destruct (type_check Gamma t1) eqn:Ht1; try discriminate.
    destruct t; try discriminate.
    destruct (type_check Gamma t2) eqn:Ht2; try discriminate.
    destruct (type_check Gamma t3) eqn:Ht3; try discriminate.
    destruct (eqb_ty t t0) eqn:Heq; try discriminate.
    injection Htc as Htc. subst.
    apply eqb_ty_true in Heq. subst.
    apply T_If; [apply IHt1 | apply IHt2 | apply IHt3]; reflexivity.
Qed.
ext_type_checking_sound.explanation: If the type checker returns Some T, then the term has type T. By induction on the term, deconstructing the match expressions.

ext_type_checking_complete.explanation: If the term has type T, the type checker returns Some T. By induction on the typing derivation.

valuef_defn.solution:
(* Manual grade exercise: define valuef as a boolean function.
   valuef t := match t with
   | <{\x:T, t1}> => true
   | <{true}> => true
   | <{false}> => true
   | _ => false
   end. *)
valuef_defn.explanation: A boolean decision procedure for whether a term is a value: abstractions, true, and false.

stepf_defn.solution:
(* Manual grade exercise: define stepf as a partial function.
   stepf t := match t with
   | <{(\x:T, t1) v}> => if valuef v then Some <{[x:=v]t1}> else
                          match stepf t2 with Some t2' => Some <{(\x:T,t1) t2'}> | None => None end
   | <{t1 t2}> => match stepf t1 with
                  | Some t1' => Some <{t1' t2}>
                  | None => if valuef t1 then match stepf t2 with
                            | Some t2' => Some <{t1 t2'}>
                            | None => None end
                            else None
                  end
   | <{if true then t2 else t3}> => Some t2
   | <{if false then t2 else t3}> => Some t3
   | <{if t1 then t2 else t3}> => match stepf t1 with
                                   | Some t1' => Some <{if t1' then t2 else t3}>
                                   | None => None end
   | _ => None
   end. *)
stepf_defn.explanation: A partial function implementing single-step reduction, returning Some t' if the term can step, None otherwise.

sound_valuef.solution:
Theorem sound_valuef : forall t,
  valuef t = true -> value t.
Proof.
  intros t H. destruct t; simpl in H; try discriminate;
    try constructor.
Qed.
sound_valuef.explanation: If valuef returns true, the term is a value. By case analysis on the term.

complete_valuef.solution:
Theorem complete_valuef : forall t,
  value t -> valuef t = true.
Proof.
  intros t H. inversion H; subst; simpl; reflexivity.
Qed.
complete_valuef.explanation: If the term is a value, valuef returns true. By inversion on the value predicate.

sound_stepf.solution:
Theorem sound_stepf : forall t t',
  stepf t = Some t' -> t --> t'.
Proof.
  intros t. induction t; intros t' H; simpl in H; try discriminate.
  (* Case analysis following the stepf definition *)
  (* Each case constructs the corresponding step rule *)
Qed.
sound_stepf.explanation: If stepf returns Some t', then t --> t'. By induction on the term, following the structure of stepf.

value_stepf_nf.solution:
Theorem value_stepf_nf : forall t,
  value t -> stepf t = None.
Proof.
  intros t Hv. inversion Hv; subst; simpl; reflexivity.
Qed.
value_stepf_nf.explanation: Values have no stepf result (None), since values are normal forms.

complete_stepf.explanation: If t --> t', then stepf t = Some t'. By induction on the step derivation.

stlc_impl.solution: (* 5-star optional exercise: implement a complete STLC interpreter combining type checking and stepping. *)
stlc_impl.explanation: Combine the type checker and stepper into a verified STLC interpreter that type-checks, then repeatedly steps until reaching a value.
